prompt:
Take the provided reference image and reproduce it PIXEL-FAITHFULLY — same overall composition (lower hand cradling the small round white device, red cylindrical button rising directly from the flat white top, upper-right hand pinch-pulling the inverted-cup-shaped cap upward off the red button, vial protruding from the device's side connector port, vertical arrow indicating up-direction), same line-art style on pure white background, same line weight, same hand poses, same cap (size and shape PERFECT — don't change), same red button (size and shape PERFECT — don't change), same hand-dominates-frame scale. DO NOT change the hands, the composition, the cap, the red button, the arrow, the line weight, or the style.

THE ONLY MODIFICATION — shorten the vial's LENGTH only: keep the vial's DIAMETER exactly as it is in the reference (don't change its width). But shorten its LENGTH — reduce the visible length of the vial protruding from the device's side port by roughly 40%, so it becomes a SHORT stubby tube rather than a long slim tube. Its length should now be roughly EQUAL to its own diameter (height ≈ diameter, instead of the previous 2x diameter). Picture: a short squat cylindrical tube — same width, just shorter. Still fully seated into the device's port (flush joint, no chrome collar visible). Still has the small double-line graduation ring (now compressed to fit on the shorter body) and empty clear-glass interior. Plain closed glass end at the far tip.

Do not modify anything else — no changes to the hands, the device, the red button, the cap, the vial's diameter, the arrow, the line weight, the background, or the style. Pure black line-art on white with the solid red raised button as the only color, no frame, no labels.
